questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The resultant displacement of a particle moving in a plane is given by the resultant of its x and y displacements.
questions.reason: Reason (R): The resultant displacement is a vector quantity and the resultant of two vectors is another vector.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The resultant displacement is indeed the resultant of the x and y displacements, and this is because the resultant displacement is a vector quantity. This makes option (A) the correct answer.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The x and y components of a vector are always perpendicular to each other.

opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The x and y components of a vector are indeed perpendicular to each other, and this is because they can be obtained by resolving the vector along two perpendicular axes. This makes option (A) the correct answer.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The x and y components of a resultant displacement are always equal in magnitude and opposite in direction.
questions.reason: Reason (R): The resultant displacement is the vector sum of its x and y components.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The x and y components of a resultant displacement are not necessarily equal in magnitude and opposite in direction. This is not a general property of resultant displacements, making option (D) the correct answer.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The time period of a particle moving in a plane is always equal to its time of flight.
questions.reason: Reason (R): The time period is the total time taken by the particle to complete one oscillation, and the time of flight is the time taken by the particle to reach the ground.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The time period of a particle moving in a plane is not necessarily equal to its time of flight. The time period is a property of the motion of the particle, while the time of flight is a property of the trajectory of the particle. This is not a general property, making option (B) the correct answer.
